steps.title: Apply the large-forest flaw
steps.content: The model's flaw depends on forest size and mammal size. For large forests, its predictions for large-mammal populations are too high. Pinehollow is a large forest, so the same large-mammal overprediction would be expected there.

steps.title: Reject unrelated flaws
steps.content: Choice A applies the small-mammal issue to a large forest. Choice C introduces predator-prey relationships, which are not discussed. Choice D says the model reflects other factors, but the stated flaw is overestimating population size. The answer is B.
